message:
feat(core,abi): port inet_nsap_addr/ntoa NSAP hex codec to core::inet::nsap (bd-nsap-epic)

Lifts ~73 lines of NSAP (Network Service Access Point) hex address
text↔binary conversion out of glibc_internal_abi.rs into a pure-safe
core::inet::nsap module. The format is a sequence of hex byte pairs
optionally separated by `.` or ` `, with an optional leading `0x` /
`0X` prefix.

New core surface (inet::nsap):
  pub fn parse_nsap_addr(text: &[u8], dst: &mut [u8]) -> usize
  pub fn format_nsap_addr(addr: &[u8]) -> Vec<u8>

parse_nsap_addr returns the byte count written, or 0 on invalid char
/ odd-digit count / empty input. format_nsap_addr produces the
canonical `xx.xx.xx...` lowercase hex form (no NUL — caller responsible
for terminating in the FFI shim).

abi changes:
  - inet_nsap_addr shim: materialize input as &CStr.to_bytes(), dst as
    &mut [u8] of buflen, call core, return the count as c_uint.
  - inet_nsap_ntoa shim: keep the static NSAP_BUF Mutex fallback for
    NULL `buf` (preserves glibc semantic), then call core for the
    formatted bytes and copy + NUL-terminate at the boundary.

Verified via rch (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc_bd-nsap-2):
  - 19 new core::inet::nsap tests pass (incl. real RFC 1629 NSAP
    example, format-then-parse round trip across 32-byte all-FF +
    256 single-byte cases, odd-digit / invalid-char rejection)
  - 173/173 abi lib tests pass
  - cargo clippy -p core -p abi -D warnings clean
  - cargo fmt --check clean

Closes bd-nsap-epic.
